publications.title: Molecular Basis of a Dominant SARS-CoV-2 Spike-Derived Epitope Presented by HLA-A*02:01 Recognised by a Public TCR.
publications.authors: Szeto C, Nguyen AT, Lobos CA, Chatzileontiadou DSM, Jayasinghe D, Grant EJ, Riboldi-Tunnicliffe A, Smith C, Gras S.
publications.executive_summary: This study determined the crystal structure of a public TCR in complex with the YLQ peptide from SARS-CoV-2 spike protein presented by HLA-A*02:01, the most common HLA allele in European populations. The structure reveals that the YLQ epitope adopts a conformationally stable binding mode upon TCR recognition, enabling high-affinity interaction and explaining why this particular TCR is shared among unrelated HLA-A*02:01+ individuals. The findings illuminate why CD8+ T cells converge on a dominant SARS-CoV-2 epitope and provide molecular detail on TCR recognition of a critical viral antigen, offering insight into protective immunity and informing rational design of T-cell-directed vaccines and therapies against COVID-19.
